us.title: Patel returns looted artifacts to Indonesian president
us.summary: A collection of cultural artifacts from Indonesia’s Papua region that were looted and illegally taken to the U.S. have been returned to Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto, Indonesian officials confirmed to reporters. The handover of the artifacts took place Wednesday at Prabowo’s residence in the Indonesian capital Jakarta during a meeting with FBI Director Kash Patel.…

us.title: Fauci to face Rand Paul in Senate hearing next week
us.summary: Dr. Anthony Fauci will testify next Wednesday in front of the Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee after being subpoenaed, chairman Rand Paul (R-Ky.) said Thursday. Paul has been laser-focused on Fauci, the nation’s former top infectious diseases doctor, for allegedly covering up the U.S.’s involvement in risky viral research in a Chinese…

us.title: Massachusetts House passes bill loosening late-term abortion restrictions
us.summary: Massachusetts state House lawmakers on Wednesday passed a bill loosening restrictions on late-term abortion access. The bill, H.5595, passed 119-33 and will go to the state Senate, state House Speaker Ronald Mariano (D) said in a statement. If it passes the Senate, it will allow an abortion after 24 weeks, if approved by a doctor.…

us.title: Trump to impose new double-digit tariffs on imports from 60 countries
us.summary: President Trump will impose double-digit tariffs on imports from 60 countries over forced labor violations, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer announced Thursday. The action will cover 99.4 percent of U.S. imports and apply to Washington’s top trading partners, according to a fact sheet released by his office. The tariffs are being brought under Section 301…

asia.title: Trump throws newly signed Saudi nuclear deal into doubt with Israel recognition demand
asia.summary: The White House threw a US-Saudi Arabia nuclear deal into question on Thursday, a day after it was signed, emphasising that it depended on the oil-rich Gulf country recognising Israel under a 2020 diplomatic initiative. “The Civil Nuclear Deal … will be approved, but is totally subject to Saudi Arabia joining the very respected and successful Abraham Accords,” US President Donald Trump wrote on social media, adding that the deal would not include enrichment and involve only non-military use of..

asia.title: Anutin stands firm on iLaw suit over poll claims
asia.summary: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has insisted he will not withdraw a defamation lawsuit against iLaw director Yingcheep Atchanont over allegations linked to the 2024 Senate election, saying legal action is the right of those affected.

asia.title: Why PLA fighter jet sorties near Taiwan dropped to a 3-year low in the first half of 2026
asia.summary: There is a change in the skies and waters around Taiwan. In the first half of this year, the number of People’s Liberation Army (PLA) fighter jets detected near the island more than halved year on year to 1,334. It was a three-year low and marked the lowest operational tempo since 2023, according to a tally by the South China Morning Post based on data from Taiwan’s defence ministry. At the same time, the number of days no PLA jets were detected by Taiwan hit a new high, and the PLA did not...

asia.title: Beyond Seoul: Can China become Asia’s next medical aesthetics hub?

tech.summary: I’ve been a NameCheap customer for 13 years. I’ve also helped out an old college club paying for a .com they use (that is registered to me under my name, address, and phone number). During a recent leadership transition, the incoming club lead wanted to make changes to the DNS and didn’t know to contact me. They figured out the domain name was parked at NameCheap, so they initiated a password reset using the domain name. I got a password reset email and immediately filed a NameCheap support tick

tech.title: Show HN: Echo – Fable-level results at 1/3 the cost using open-weight models
tech.summary: I’ve been building Echo (https://echo.tracerml.ai/), an experiment in making one AI system out of a pool of open-weight models rather than choosing a single model and using it for every task.It started with a simple experiment. I took a group of models, including GLM-5.2, Kimi K2.7 and others, and ran them on the same evaluations. Then I measured what would happen if, for each problem, you somehow knew in advance which models would be useful and how their outputs should be combined.That hypothet

briefing: Global markets are on high alert as international benchmark oil prices have crossed $100 per barrel, while President Trump is set to impose double-digit tariffs on imports from 60 countries over forced labor violations, affecting 99.4% of US imports. In Singapore, GIC's 20-year annualised real return has fallen to 3.4%, its lowest in six years, as the sovereign wealth fund prioritises resilience over risk, and the local stock market is seeing underwhelming IPO performances, with six listings this year trading below their issue price. Meanwhile, in the tech sphere, Amazon is launching an AI update for its Alexa Plus assistant to handle more complicated instructions and connect to smart home devices in new ways.

digests.singapore: Singapore's economic and financial landscape is experiencing challenges, with GIC's 20-year annualised real return falling to its lowest in six years and recent IPOs trading below issue price, reflecting thin liquidity and a global shift towards AI stocks. Meanwhile, the government is taking steps to support workers amidst AI disruption, with Ng Chee Meng's return to the Cabinet expected to give workers a stronger voice. Other local issues include the need for transparency in the resignation of a minister, the impact of drug abuse on families and friends, and service disruptions affecting financial services such as DBS PayLah!.
digests.us: The US government is taking actions on multiple fronts, including the return of looted cultural artifacts to Indonesia and the imposition of double-digit tariffs on imports from 60 countries over forced labor violations. Dr. Anthony Fauci is set to face questioning from Senator Rand Paul in a Senate hearing, while Massachusetts lawmakers have passed a bill loosening late-term abortion restrictions. Additionally, a new study has found a significant increase in gig economy workers relying on federal benefits, highlighting the ongoing impact of the pandemic on the US economy.
digests.asia: Protests and political tensions are escalating in India and Thailand, with Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ending a hunger strike over leaked medical school entrance exam papers, while Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul is pursuing a defamation lawsuit against a critic. Meanwhile, geopolitical developments are unfolding in West Asia, with oil prices surging past $100 per barrel amid Houthi attacks and a US-Saudi nuclear deal hanging in the balance, contingent on Saudi recognition of Israel. Regional military postures are also shifting, with a notable decline in PLA fighter jet sorties near Taiwan, marking a three-year low.
digests.others: Protests have erupted in Italy following the death of a Moroccan-born man after being restrained by police, sparking comparisons to the George Floyd case, while in the Middle East, tensions between the US and Iran continue to escalate with the US threatening further strikes and seeking additional funding for its military efforts. Meanwhile, India and Ukraine are experiencing internal power struggles, with an activist ending a 26-day hunger strike and Ukraine's ousted defence minister refusing to accept an alternative post. Sweden is taking steps to reduce its reliance on China by easing mining permits for critical minerals and rare earths, declaring it a national security interest.
digests.tech: Recent technology and innovation headlines highlight advancements in AI-powered smart home devices, such as Amazon's Alexa Plus update, which enables more complex interactions with various smart home devices. However, concerns over data security and privacy have also emerged, as seen in the case of Namecheap handing over account control to an unverified third party, and the installation of McAfee ads on Windows through LG monitors. Additionally, the use of gene-editing therapies has raised questions about their safety and efficacy, following the death of a girl who received a costly treatment.
digests.economist: The Economist's analysis highlights geopolitical tensions and power shifts, including China's growing military capabilities through its partnership with Russia, and the ongoing struggles for power and influence in countries such as Lebanon, the Philippines, and Indonesia. Meanwhile, economic trends are also shifting, with investors losing interest in traditional European defence firms and instead turning to newer technologies like drone warfare, while manufacturers find new revenue streams in areas like airplane-engine maintenance. The intersection of politics and technology is also evident in the increasing importance of access to air-conditioning as a geopolitical issue amidst rising global temperatures.
